3. Retrieval Gate Localization (Ingestion / Recall / Rerank)

Specific docs questions traced through the retrieval gates. target_sim = similarity of the best index chunk to the ground-truth answer; loss_stage = first gate where that chunk was lost (ingestion → not well represented in any chunk; recall → never entered the fused pool; rerank → cut by the cross-encoder).

174 reached answer (86.6%)
8 lost @ recall (4.0%)
19 lost @ rerank (9.5%)
0 lost @ ingestion (0.0%)
IDQuestionCandidateCategory target_simIn IndexIn PoolIn FinalLoss Stage

p001 project What is this project? The project is called the **Candidate AI Agent**, an AI-powered tool designed to act as a job candid... It is an AI-powered digital representative for a job candidate: a recruiter chats with it over a sec... 0.800
p002 project Give me a high-level overview of the system. The Candidate AI Agent is a system designed to streamline recruiter-candidate interactions by levera... An agentic recruiter-facing chat that routes between verified structured facts, a trained 1-5 skill-... 0.600
p003 project What reranker does the project use? The project uses a **cross-encoder reranker** for re-ranking, with two pluggable options: 1. **Voya... A cross-encoder reranker: the Voyage rerank API by default, or a local Qwen3-Reranker, which is a ca... 0.800
p004 project How does the skill proficiency estimation work? The skill proficiency estimation works through a dedicated NLP model trained to score skills on a 1–... A fine-tuned DeBERTa-v3 transformer with a CORAL ordinal regression head scores each listed skill fr... 0.500
p005 project What evaluation frameworks does the project use? The project's evaluation frameworks are primarily divided into two phases: 1. **Build Phase**: Foc... RAGAS for RAG quality (faithfulness, context precision and recall, answer relevancy) and DeepEval's ... 0.300
p006 project What is Reciprocal Rank Fusion used for in the ret Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) is used in the hybrid fusion retrieval stage of the RAG pipeline to com... RRF merges the ranked result lists from dense vector search and BM25 keyword search (across the expa... 0.900
p007 project Which vector database does the project use? The project uses **ChromaDB** as its vector database. It serves as a persistent on-disk index with c... ChromaDB, used as a persistent on-disk index with cosine similarity, with a separate collection per ... 0.800
p008 project What embedding options does the project support? The project supports two embedding options: 1. **Voyage AI API** (production default) 2. **Local ... Voyage AI embeddings as the cloud default, or a local nomic-embed-text model via sentence-transforme... 1.000
p009 project How are documents ingested? Documents are ingested through a structured pipeline designed for efficient parsing, chunking, and m... They are parsed with the Unstructured library (PyMuPDF for PDFs, python-docx for Word, Tesseract OCR... 0.800
p010 project What tools does the agent have? The agent has four core tools, organized into two categories: **candidate-focused tools** and **proj... Four tools: get_structured_data for verified facts, get_skill_proficiency for the 1-5 trained skill ... 1.000
p011 project What does the query router do? The query router in the Candidate AI Agent system directs incoming user questions to the appropriate... An LLM router classifies each query as BROAD (a general overview question) or SPECIFIC; broad querie... 0.000
p012 project How is the recruiter chat deployed and secured? The recruiter chat is deployed on a **Streamlit-based host** (e.g., Streamlit Community Cloud or Hug... It deploys to a Streamlit host such as Streamlit Community Cloud or Hugging Face Spaces over HTTPS, ... 0.900
p013 project What is the build-vs-serve split? The **build-vs-serve split** is a core design principle of the project, separating heavy processing ... The build phase runs locally and parses documents, embeds them, and runs the skill scorer to produce... 0.900
p014 project What do the 1 to 5 skill proficiency levels mean? The 1 to 5 skill proficiency levels represent the candidate's mastery of a specific skill, as inferr... 1 is awareness (a passing mention), 2 is working familiarity, 3 is competent or day-to-day use, 4 is... 1.000
p015 project How was the training data for the skill scorer cre The training data for the skill scorer was created using a **synthetic data generation system** desi... It was synthetically generated: realistic candidate personas and archetypes with controlled skill ev... 0.800
p016 project Which LLM does the project use? The project uses the **Anthropic Claude API** (production default) or a **local Ollama model** as th... Claude via the Anthropic API by default, or a local Ollama model; the choice is configurable, and th... 0.900
p017 project What is the per-gate retrieval analysis? The **per-gate retrieval analysis** is a diagnostic feature of the evaluation suite designed to iden... For specific document questions it localizes where a relevant chunk was lost in retrieval — at inges... 0.700
p018 project How many synthetic candidates are used for evaluat The evaluation uses **6 synthetic candidates**, each with a golden dataset of **75 questions**. Thes... Six synthetic candidates with varied document formats, each with a golden dataset of 75 questions.... 0.800
p019 project What hybrid search methods are combined in retriev The retrieval pipeline combines the following hybrid search methods: 1. **Dense Vector Search**: Us... Dense vector search over ChromaDB embeddings and sparse BM25 Okapi keyword search, fused with Recipr... 1.000
p020 project Does the project use prompt caching? The project does use prompt caching. According to the documentation, prompt caching is applied to th... Yes. Prompt caching is applied to the frozen system prompt and the tool definitions to reduce the co... 1.000
